key_insight: High-velocity air fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality after annealing.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to add functional, self-actuating NiTi surfaces to steel components for wear-resistant, adaptive, and thermally responsive applications.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi coatings onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Characterized chemistry, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation behavior, and thermomechanical response
Compared as-sprayed and annealed coatings under thermal and mechanical loading
evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated by microstructural and phase analyses plus 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests, showing martensitic transformation and thermal actuation despite defects.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are not fully functional; annealing is required, and the coatings still show inhomogeneous microstructure, oxides, voids, and limited tensile strength.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
